paper_title: A Wheeled Wall-Climbing Robot with Bio-Inspired Spine Mechanisms
source_file: 机理文献/条件备选/06_A Wheeled Wall-Climbing Robot with Bio-Inspired Spine Mechanisms.pdf

render_review.all_pages: All 12 PDF pages were rendered to PNG at 120 dpi and visually inspected. The paper title, two-column reading order, all section boundaries, figures, tables, conclusions and reference boundary were checked.
render_review.notes: Retained equations, stiffness-matrix units, force directions, fitted c values, Table 4 values and all three figure crops were checked against page renders rather than text extraction alone.

sections_checked: Section 2 Robot design, Sections 2.1 Mechanical design and 2.2 Bio-inspired spine mechanism
Section 3 Modeling and simulation, Sections 3.1 Quasi-static equilibrium analysis, 3.2 Multi-spine/surface interaction simulation model and 3.3 Simulation results
Section 4 Experiments, Sections 4.1 Spine/surface interaction testing and 4.2 Climbing experiments
Acknowledgment and references checked for document completeness only

equations_checked.notes: The 3x3 stiffness-matrix arrangement, x/y/theta order and anticlockwise theta direction were checked against Fig. 3 and the rendered equation. Retained in E1.

The matrix relation K(delta1 delta2 delta3)=(F1 F2 F3) and the three applied displacement vectors were checked. The evidence card reports it as the FEA identification procedure rather than silently treating the non-unit displacement columns as identity.
FA, FT and T expressions, all sin/cos terms, mu1, L and r factors were checked against the page render and Fig. 5. Retained in E2.
K0=T_alpha0^T K T_alpha0, Fm=Km delta_m, Km=T_m^T K0 T_m, the m Delta alpha rotation matrix and all signs were checked. Retained in E3.
Delta r=Delta x_m-Delta u_m and m Delta H=Delta y_m-Delta v_m were checked with Fig. 7b. Retained in E4.

The three scalar compatibility/equilibrium rows for the m-th spine were inspected and used to interpret Eq. (18), but omitted from the compact card because E3-E4 already preserve the transferable structure.
The factor of two for symmetric wheels, sums from i=1 to m, and M=2 sum F_ix(i Delta H-|R| cos beta) were checked. Retained in E5.

The (3m+2)-dimensional unknown vector, block coefficient matrix, right-hand side, FA/2 and FT/2 closure rows were visually checked at 300 dpi. The full block matrix is deliberately omitted from the evidence card because it hard-codes the paper's flat-wall, fixed-pin and equal-spacing assumptions; the assembly structure remains traceable through Eqs. (6)-(17).

The printed c=tan(theta_min-tan^-1(1/mu)) and F_mx<cF_my inequality are legible and were transcribed exactly. However, the adjacent text describing when the force is outside the constraint does not uniquely resolve the admissible side/equality event. The evidence card flags this as non-executable without re-derivation.

tables_checked.notes: PA2200 and stainless-steel density, Young's modulus, Poisson ratio and tensile strength were visually checked. Only modulus and tensile strength are retained as reproduction-relevant values.
Both 3x3 stiffness matrices and the mixed unit matrix were checked at 300 dpi. All nine entries of each matrix retained in E1 match the rendered table.
Wheel/tail mass, length, width, wheel diameter and floor/brick speeds were checked. Values used in the card are separated from model inputs.
Tail lengths 100/110/120/200 mm and simulation/experiment phi_max pairs 87/85, 99/95, 103/100 and 105/100 degrees were checked.

figures_checked.reason: Robot T-layout, wheel width, spacers and circular spine arrangement were inspected. The 16x4 hierarchy and dimensions are retained textually; later force/geometry figures are more mechanism-dense.
Biological tarsus images were inspected as design inspiration but contain no transferable force, stiffness or failure data.

Irreplaceable definition of the x/y/theta frame, wheel-driven suspension deformation, S-shaped versus straight suspension geometry, and dimensions supporting E1. The crop includes both figures and complete captions.
Combined crop preserves whole-robot/tail force directions and the multi-spine active-contact set used by E2 and E5. Both captions and all labels were visually verified.
The alpha0, Delta x_m, Delta y_m, R and beta geometry was inspected at 300 dpi. E3-E4 and the source pointer preserve the needed relation without a fourth image.

The bio-inspired preload/adhesive/pull-off sequence and the regular design's missing preload phase are unambiguous in text and simulation values; the plot is not needed once the state sequence is retained.
The phi_max-versus-c curves for four weights were inspected. They are simulation-only and the transferable non-monotonic weight warning is represented textually.
The two-load-cell fixture was inspected. Instrument identity and measured components are sufficient; the hardware photograph is not required for model transfer.
Direct brick evidence for the angle-dependent Fx/Fy constraint. The crop preserves all scatter points, axes, units, four fitted slopes and the complete caption.
Video stills confirm floor-to-wall transition on brick and pearl brocade but do not validate force histories. The surface-specific performance and mode boundary are retained textually.

omitted_sections: General survey of suction, adhesives, magnets and prior climbing robots in the Introduction
Detailed biological image description beyond the compliant-chain design analogy
Ordinary prototype fabrication and actuator details not affecting the retained models

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.7, Section 3.2, Eq. (19) and adjacent prose
uncertain_items.item: Admissible side of the pull-off constraint
uncertain_items.detail: The formula and inequality are legible, but the wording about being outside F_mx<cF_my does not uniquely define the release event. No executable inequality direction is inferred.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.3 and p.6-8, Sections 2.1 and 3.2-3.3
uncertain_items.item: Mapping of physical 16x4 array to model n=64
uncertain_items.detail: The hardware is described as 16 arrays across wheel width with four spines each, while the 2D model uses n=64 equally spaced angular rows. The phase/width reduction is not explained.

uncertain_items.item: Pull-off trial count and uncontrolled history variables
uncertain_items.detail: The text says several trials but gives no sample count, variance or raw data. Approaching angle, dragging distance and preload are explicitly ignored.
Whole-robot climbable angle trends are compared, but no per-spine force, active contact count or Eq. (18) load distribution is experimentally measured.

known_warnings: The paper provides no measured brick topography, friction coefficient, local strength or damage threshold; brick performance cannot be interpreted as a pure morphology effect.
The 3x3 stiffness matrices are FEA outputs for the paper's PA2200 geometries and were not validated by physical stiffness tests.
The multi-spine solver assumes fixed pin contacts, no sliding before disengagement, small linear suspension deflection and identical local stiffness after rotation.
The 2D model collapses wheel width, lateral staggering, contact competition and left-right asymmetry.
The symbol M denotes the aggregate spine-system moment, while M_m denotes a local spine-tip moment set to zero; these must not be conflated.
The fitted coefficient c is angle- and protocol-dependent and must not be used as a substrate material constant.
Pearl-brocade results arise from penetration and cannot be transferred to hard-brick asperity interlocking.
The floor-to-wall transition is demonstrated by video frames but is outside the quasi-static model's validated scope.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs after excluding display-formula blocks, Markdown table rows and image-link rows.
